questions.question: Which of the following companies did Elon Musk co-found or found, then later sold or merged, eventually leading to PayPal?
questions.explanation: Musk co-founded X.com, an online payment company that later merged to form PayPal, which was acquired by eBay.

questions.question: In what year did Elon Musk become an American citizen?
questions.explanation: The material states, 'Musk also became an American citizen in 2002.'

questions.question: As of February 2026, what was Forbes' estimate of Elon Musk's net worth?
questions.explanation: As of February 2026, Forbes estimates his net worth to be around US$852 billion.

questions.question: Elon Musk co-founded OpenAI in 2015, but later left. What was the primary reason cited for his departure?
options: He wanted to focus solely on Tesla.
He disagreed with the company's decision to go public.
Growing discontent with the organization's direction and their leadership in the AI boom.
He was asked to leave due to conflicts of interest.
questions.explanation: Growing discontent with the organization's direction and their leadership in the AI boom in the 2020s led him to establish xAI.

questions.question: What significant change did Elon Musk make to the social network Twitter after acquiring it in 2022?
questions.explanation: In 2022, he acquired the social network Twitter, implementing significant changes, and rebranding it as X in 2023.

questions.question: Which of the following citizenships did Elon Musk hold from birth?
questions.explanation: Musk holds both South African and Canadian citizenship from birth because his mother was born in Canada.

questions.question: After his parents divorced, Elon, at around age 9, chose to live with his father. What was his primary reason for this decision?
options: His father lived in a bigger house.
His father had an Encyclopædia Britannica and a computer.
His father offered him more freedom.
His mother was frequently away for modeling.
questions.explanation: Elon, aged around 9, chose to live with his father because Errol Musk had an Encyclopædia Britannica and a computer.

questions.question: Elon Musk served in the Trump administration after Donald Trump's inauguration in early 2025. What was his primary role?
options: Senior Advisor to the President and de facto head of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)
questions.explanation: After Trump was inaugurated as president in early 2025, Musk served as Senior Advisor to the President and as the de facto head of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).

questions.question: At what age did Elon Musk sell his first BASIC-based game, Blastar?
questions.explanation: At age twelve, Elon sold his BASIC-based game Blastar to PC and Office Technology magazine.

questions.question: Which of the following is cited as a criticism against Elon Musk in the provided material?
options: His investment in reusable rocket technology for SpaceX.
His role as CEO of Tesla.
COVID-19 misinformation, promoting conspiracy theories, and affirming antisemitic, racist, and transphobic comments.
questions.explanation: Musk has been criticized for COVID-19 misinformation, promoting conspiracy theories, and affirming antisemitic, racist, and transphobic comments.
